Tragacanth is a plant-based adhesive derived from the sap of the Astragalus plant, primarily the species Astragalus gummifer. It is a complex mixture of polysaccharides and mucilages, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its soothing and protective properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include astragalosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Tragacanth is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as powders, syrups, and ointments, and is often combined with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
